title: lithocarpus paviei
intro: Lithocarpus paviei, also known as the Vietnamese tanbark oak, is a species of evergreen tree in the beech family Fagaceae. Traditionally, Lithocarpus paviei is used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The plant is valued for its health benefits, such as its ability to treat fever, respiratory issues, and digestive problems, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Lithocarpus paviei can be prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional Vietnamese medicine to promote overall health and well-being.
